response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior developer with broad full-stack experience and strong fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of req/s), but the resume shows no modern C++ or Rust experience in the last decade and no geospatial routing/navigation background. GIS work exists via GISCollective but it's web-based mapping in DLang/Node, not routing tile pipelines. The C/C++ stint at National Instruments was brief and over 10 years ago.
strengths: Distributed/low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (high-throughput services, async I/O, caching)
Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective and Green Map open-source work
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, trunk-based dev, CI/CD, testing focus
concerns: No recent modern C++ (last C/C++ role was 2014-2015 at NI) and no Rust experience listed
No navigation/routing tile pipeline experience — GIS work is map collection, not routing
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/AWS, a significant shift away from the systems-level C++ role
